title: Week 10 Review
html:
Congratulations on completing week ten! After finishing your lessons you should now know:

  • 9 new kanji
  • 12 new words in katakana
  • How to use question words + verb in plain form + か, use 個 (ko) counter for things and 泊 (haku) counter for nights of stay, use 何か国 (nankakoku) and count countries, use the verb in plain form + んです (i.e. 話してるんです) and use a verb in plain form +ですが
  • How to use a verb in plain form + でしょう to ask for approval, use a question word + verb in た form + らいいですか to seek instruction from the listener, use a verb in た form + どうですか to give advice, and use a verb in て form + いただけませんか to say “could you please”
  • Noun 1 + Noun 2 + が + Verb in potential form
  • Verbs in potential form, verb in てform + みます to express “trying to do something”, and verb in plain form + かどうか  to express “whether or not”
  • How to use 5 new grammar points:
    • Grammar focus on the use of はっきり
    • Grammar focus on ずいぶん、実は (jitsu wa) and それで
    • Grammar focus on the use of は to express contrast, and the particles も and しか
    • Grammar focus on が to introduce another sentence, けど and なお
    • Grammar focus on なかなか and こんな／そんな／あんな
If you have any questions on what you have learned this week, don’t forget that you can always ask for more help in the student community!
